title: torularia torulosa var. hispida
intro: Torularia torulosa var. hispida, also known as Indian dodder or yellow dodder, is a type of parasitic flowering plant that obtains its nutrients by connecting to and tapping the sap of other plants. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiviral, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Torularia torulosa var. hispida include its potential to lower blood sugar levels, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in traditional Indian medicine, Ayurveda, to treat various ailments.
